replies.text: @Jonathonconroy has to make close to a billion to break even.

replies.author: @user-mj7gm6gj9v
replies.text: @wstml555 I'm estimating well OVER a billion. With 400 million invested in the movie and at least 150 million in promotions, it would have to make 1.1 billion in the US alone where it gets 50% of the box office. If it's a 50/50 split between US and international box offices, the movie would have to earn 550 million in the US (to recoup 275 mil) and 915 million internationally (to recoup the other 275 mil). Worldwide, the movie would have to make 1.4 billion. And that's only IF Disney isn't lying about the actual cost invested.

author: @TinaBiebe
text:
Good grief. I’ve already forgotten about Captain Communist. 😂 Snow White should be down my memory hole a few days after it opens. 

My only hope is that Fathom Events will have a few great movies that might interest me. At this rate the last movie I saw theatrically was Fathom’s 85th anniversary celebration of Gone With The Wind last summer. Otherwise nothing else has me excited. I used to love going to the movies. We saw even bad movies cause tickets were so cheap. Times have changed. If I don’t see it theatrically, I likely won’t bother streaming it either cause nothing about it interested me. I wish Hollywood could go back to making real movies again but the obsession with super heroes has gutted me. I’m tired of them and I know that’s not an overwhelming opinion, but if I never see another one I’m okay. I’m tired of the Message, the ugly actors, the politics when they should shut up. By the time they run through all the stuff they green-lit a few years ago it’ll be too late anyway. Sorry for the pessimism but I’ll just stay home and throw The Godfather or Ben-Hur or LOTR in my Blu-ray and relax at home. Sadly, Hollywood hasn’t made it worth it seeing their current stuff.

author: @josephbrown9685
text: I have a modest home theater that didn’t cost a ton of money where I can enjoy thousands of movies and video games. Why would I want to spend a lot of money to go to a theater for mediocre modern Hollywood entertainment?  I’d say many people are in the same situation.

author: @hittrewweuy7595
text: Personally I am done with all garbage released , huge prices at the theater , too crowded , too stinky ….. is so much better watch movies at home on a good screen with bathroom breaks or replays etc. theaters will never see any of my money anymore

author: @drewwn
text:
Proof that the professional "Story Tellers", no longer know how to entertain a paying audience with a story.  They've also forgotten or, don't care, that the industry they have chosen to work in, is apart of Mass Media, specifically; Popular Culture.  It's not all that difficult for people that know how it's done.  And it's been done since long before recorded history.
These mental pygmies are trying to apply a lot of nonsense they were taught in college.  Such as; cast against type, the story calls for a tall blond male, cast a black female instead.  Don't make the villain entirely evil, no, instead make the villain sympathetic and how he/she was horrible wronged by the protagonist.  The story delivered is basically sound and makes sense; oh no, that won't do, that's what the audience is expecting and we can't do that, we must subvert the audience's expectations.
Please, keep up the bad work Hollywood, and show us all how not to tell a story.

text: Too many schlocky superhero movies. How about movies about ordinary people, individually and collectively, saving the world from predatory governments and corporations?

author: @norbertthetanksantos
text: Movie theaters are going to go under. Good movies are no longer making good movies with a good story good actors with no political or woke garbage to spend the money to go . Eventually going the route of straight to streaming is the way it will go .

author: @Rainy_Day12234
text: Disney sucks

author: @Number-Forty-Two
text: I'm amazed that any cinema's are still active at all, as since Endgame any movies that have not been completely destroyed by wokeness can be counted on the fingers of one hand. Since 2012 I've been to the cinema just five times, one of which was to take my great niece to see the ruined Little Mermaid, after 20 minutes she wanted to leave saying that Ariel "was not the Little Mermaid.",  The other times I went to see Top Gun: Maverick, Guardians of the Galaxy 3, Dead Reckoning Part One and Dune: Parts One and Two Double Feature. I used to go once every two or three months before the infection of identity politics literally destroyed the desire. To be honest I can never see that changing, either.

replies.text: For the giant screen and the sound. No one has that at their house. We still go 2-3x a week and we have access to everything streaming. Seeing a film in the theater is a captivating experience.

text: We saw "Opus" this wknd. It was good. During the week we'll be seeing "Black Bag" and the Looney Tunes film. The movies are good, and it feels like our culture is just changing toward small screen and couch watching.
